prompt_text: Suggest a weekly workout plan for someone who cannot go to the gym and can only exercise at home in a small space.
response_text:
Here’s a weekly workout plan that can be done at home in a small space, requiring minimal equipment (like a yoga mat and optional dumbbells or resistance bands). This plan includes a mix of strength training, cardio, and flexibility exercises.

### Weekly Workout Plan

#### Day 1: Full Body Strength
- Warm-up (5-10 minutes): Jumping jacks or high knees
- Circuit (repeat 2-3 times):
  - Bodyweight Squats: 15 reps
  - Push-ups (knee or standard): 10-15 reps
  - Dumbbell or Bodyweight Shoulder Press: 10-12 reps
  - Glute Bridges: 15 reps
  - Plank: 30 seconds to 1 minute
- Cool down (5-10 minutes): Stretching (focus on all major muscle groups)

#### Day 2: Cardio & Core
- Warm-up (5-10 minutes): March in place or dynamic stretching
- Circuit (repeat 2-3 times):
  - Burpees: 10 reps
  - Mountain Climbers: 30 seconds
  - Russian Twists: 15 reps (each side)
  - Leg Raises: 10-15 reps
  - High Knees: 30 seconds
- Cool down (5-10 minutes): Stretching

#### Day 3: Lower Body Focus
- Warm-up (5-10 minutes): Light jogging in place or dynamic leg swings
- Circuit (repeat 2-3 times):
  - Lunges (alternating legs): 10-12 reps per leg
  - Calf Raises: 15-20 reps
  - Side Leg Raises: 10-15 reps per leg
  - Donkey Kicks: 10-15 reps per leg
  - Wall Sit: 30 seconds to 1 minute
- Cool down (5-10 minutes): Stretching (focus on legs and hips)

#### Day 4: Active Recovery
- Choose a light activity that you enjoy (e.g., walking, yoga, or gentle stretching).
- Duration: 20-30 minutes

#### Day 5: Upper Body & Core
- Warm-up (5-10 minutes): Arm circles and torso twists
- Circuit (repeat 2-3 times):
  - Tricep Dips (using a sturdy chair): 10-12 reps
  - Bent-over Dumbbell Rows: 10-12 reps
  - Plank Shoulder Taps: 10 taps per side
  - Bicycle Crunches: 15 reps per side
  - Side Plank: 20-30 seconds each side
- Cool down (5-10 minutes): Stretching (focus on arms, shoulders, and core)

#### Day 6: HIIT (High-Intensity Interval Training)
- Warm-up (5-10 minutes): Jumping jacks or dynamic stretching
- Circuit (20 seconds on, 10 seconds off, repeat for 15-20 minutes):
  - High Knees
  - Push-ups
  - Jump Squats
  - Burpees
  - Plank Jacks
- Cool down (5-10 minutes): Stretching

#### Day 7: Flexibility & Recovery
- Focus on stretching and flexibility.
- Spend 20-30 minutes doing yoga or deep stretching exercises.
- Consider using online resources or apps for guided sessions.

### Tips:
- Adjust repetitions and sets based on your fitness level.
- Stay hydrated and listen to your body; it's okay to take breaks as needed.
- Consider tracking your progress to stay motivated.

This plan is designed to be flexible, so feel free to mix and match days based on how you’re feeling or your schedule!
